qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Low-privilege formula validation DoS via unbounded Twig range evaluation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an authenticated formula-validation denial-of-service in FormulasController/Formulas, while the advisory fixes stored XSS caused by missing HTML encoding in InventoryController's inventory table rendering for purchasable description and SKU fields.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a stored XSS in Commerce inventory-page rendering, but the only qualifying finding concerns Twig range-based memory exhaustion in formula validation endpoints.
evidence: The advisory text and fix commit 9f0638a4fb29ed8295a463385a7cc49ec986e33a point to stored XSS on the Commerce inventory page and add Html::encode() in src/controllers/InventoryController.php when rendering purchasable descriptions and SKU values.

The only qualifying finding report names POST /actions/commerce/formulas/validate-condition and /validate-formula, with code locations in FormulasController.php and Formulas.php, and describes unbounded Twig range() evaluation leading to memory exhaustion.
These paths do not share the same function, endpoint, sink, missing guard, or rendering logic, so the finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause.
